name: Steamed Persimmon Pudding with Brandy Sauce
recipeInstructions:
To prepare the pudding, butter the lid and sides of a 2-quart pudding mold (about 2 tbsp). Dust with about 2 tbsp sugar. Prepare a heavy, deep pot for steaming by placing a trivet or small rack on the bottom. Place the unfilled mold on the rack and pour in enough water to come halfway up the mold. Remove the mold and bring water to a simmer while preparing the pudding.
Peel persimmons and puree in a food processor or blender. Combine raisins and plump in brandy. Place persimmon puree in a large mixing bowl, add sugar and butter, and beat together.
Sift flour, baking soda, salt, and spices into a small bowl.
Fold the dry ingredients into persimmon puree by hand, mixing well. Add pecans and raisin mixture. Gradually stir in cream, mixing thoroughly. Whip egg whites to soft peaks. Fold them into the pudding and pour into the prepared mold. Secure the lid.
Tap the pudding on the counter several times to break up any air bubbles. Carefully lower the mold into the simmering water, placing it in the center of the pot so steam circulates around the mold. Cover the pot with a lid. Simmer for 2-2.5 hours over low heat, checking to maintain an even water level while steaming the pudding. The pudding is done when a skewer inserted into it comes out almost clean. Cool in the pan until the pudding shrinks from the sides of the pan, about 15 minutes. Invert onto a serving platter.
Prepare Brandy Sauce by melting butter and sugar together in the top of a double boiler. When melted, cook for an additional minute. Cool slightly. Beat egg in a small bowl, then whisk it into the melted mixture. Whisk in brandy. Serve immediately or keep warm over hot water.
